title: SDS PAGE
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"i3cr","text":"Prepare your gel casting rig. You can use water to test for leaks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ch4b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"32esk","text":"On your glass plate, mark a line 1 cm below where the bottom of your comb will be. This is the line that you will fill your separating gel to.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"cohuk","text":"Unpolymerised acrylamide is a neurotoxin. Early signs of exposure include numbness, tingling, and tenderness to touch. Symptoms can be delayed several days to weeks and if exposure continues (even in small quantities), other symptoms may arise including excessive sweating, blue-reddish skin, peeling of skin, and weakness in limbs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c4isd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6gfme","text":"Wear gloves and work in a fume hood. Unpolymerised acrylamide can pass through unbroken skin. In the case of skin contact, remove any contaminated clothing and rinse the affected area with water and soap for at least 15 minutes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7f6kr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eoknh","text":"Polymerised acrylamide is not toxic.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: This recipe is enough to cast two gels. Add the following to a 15mL tube: 4.1 mL, dH2O 2.5 mL, 1.5 M Tris (pH 8.8) 3.3 mL, Acrylamide (30%) 50 µL, SDS

When you are ready to cast the separating gel, add 100 µL, Ammonium persulphate (10% w/v) and 10 µL, TEMED in quick succession. This will start the polymerisation. Invert tube a few times to mix, but do not shake. Pipette the solution between the glass plates up to your fill line. Tap on the benchtop to remove any bubbles, and overlay with a layer of ethanol or isopropanol. Once the gel has polymerised, discard the alcohol layer and rinse with running buffer or water. Use filter paper to remove excess from the top of the gel before pouring the stacking gel.
This recipe is enough stacking gel for two gels. Add the following to a 15mL tube: 2.7 mL, dH2O 0.5 mL, 0.5 M Tris (pH 6.8) 0.8 mL, Acrylamide (30%) 20 µL, SDS

When you are ready to cast the stacking gel, add 40 µL, Ammonium persulphate (10% w/v) and 4 µL, TEMED in quick succession. This will start the polymerisation. Pipette the solution between the glass plates and insert the comb. Check for any bubbles.
creator: Shafik Oubihi
